address: Niphat Uthit 3 Road, Hat Yai District

description.en:
## Overview

The ASEAN Night Bazaar is Hat Yai's largest and most organized night market, located on Niphat Uthit 3 Road in the heart of the city. This modern covered market complex spreads across two levels and draws a lively mix of Thai locals, Malaysian day-trippers, and international tourists looking for bargain shopping, street food, and an authentic southern Thai evening out. Unlike open-air night markets that suffer in the rain, the ASEAN Night Bazaar's covered structure means it operates comfortably regardless of weather.

## Shopping

The two-level market offers an enormous selection of goods at bargain prices. The ground floor focuses on clothing, shoes, bags, and accessories, with both new and second-hand items available at prices well below mall retail. Upstairs you will find souvenirs, handicrafts, electronics accessories, and miscellaneous goods from across the ASEAN region. Bargaining is expected and encouraged, especially for clothing and souvenirs, and prices are generally very reasonable even before negotiating. The market is particularly popular with Malaysian visitors who cross the border specifically to shop here, taking advantage of the favorable exchange rate.

## Food and Dining

The food section of the bazaar is a highlight in itself, with dozens of stalls serving southern Thai street food at rock-bottom prices. Must-try dishes include roti mataba, grilled seafood skewers, mango sticky rice, and the region's famous spicy fried chicken. Thai milk tea and fresh fruit smoothies are popular drinks to cool down with. Most food items cost between 30 and 80 THB. The central dining area has shared seating where you can mix and match dishes from different vendors.

## Practical Information

The ASEAN Night Bazaar is open Tuesday through Sunday from 4:30 PM to 10:00 PM and is closed on Mondays. There is no entrance fee. The peak hours are between 6:00 and 9:00 PM when the market is most lively. The bazaar is within walking distance of most Hat Yai hotels in the city center. Bring cash in small denominations as most vendors do not accept cards. The market is easily combined with a stroll through Hat Yai's other nearby night markets for a full evening of shopping and eating.
description.nl: Grote nachtmarkt met winkelen dineren en entertainment voor locals en toeristen

best_time_to_visit: Between 6:00 and 9:00 PM for peak atmosphere; Tuesday through Sunday only (closed Mondays)
tips: Bring cash in small denominations as most vendors do not accept cards or mobile payments
Bargain on clothing and souvenirs where prices drop 20-30% with friendly negotiation
The market is closed on Mondays so plan your Hat Yai evening activities accordingly
